Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:

Examples:

    "symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#LoopAuditEvent:struct",
    "symbol:crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs#AuditSink:trait"
  ],
  "source": "impl AuditSink for InMemorySink {\n    fn emit(&self, event: &LoopAuditEvent) {\n        self.events.lock().expect(\"audit mutex\").push(event.clone());\n    }\n    fn write_blob(&self, content: &[u8]) -> String {\n        let hash = self\n            .blob_store\n            .write(content)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|err| format!(\"error:{err}\"));\n        self.blobs\n            .lock()\n            .expect(\"blob mutex\")\n            .push((hash.clone(), content.to_vec()));\n        hash\n    }\n}"
}

item.text: I’ve confirmed the trait definition and the concrete sink impl blocks in `audit/mod.rs`. One last targeted query will enumerate all `AuditSink` implementors from the graph so I can make sure there aren’t any elsewhere in the workspace.

item.text:
## A. PolicyDenial constructors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/agent_loop.rs`: `AgentLoop::run` emits `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` when a requested tool is not in the loop allowlist, then returns `AgentLoopError::PolicyDenied`.
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink::emit` synthesizes a `LoopAuditEvent::PolicyDenial { ... }` when it observes a disallowed `ToolCallRequested` event and records the denial through the inner sink.

## B. AuditSink implementors
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `NullSink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `InMemorySink`
- `crates/orbit-agent/src/loop_engine/audit/mod.rs`: `JsonlFileSink`
- `crates/orbit-engine/src/activity_job/tool_enforcement.rs`: `EnforcedAuditSink`
